---
title: SenNet
alias: &ALIAS sennet
href: https://sennetconsortium.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

SenNet (Cellular Senescence Network) is an NIH Common Fund program creating comprehensive maps of senescent cells across the human body to understand their role in aging and disease.

## Key Features

- Generates single-cell, spatial, and imaging data to map senescent cell states across tissues
- Develops biomarkers and molecular signatures of cellular senescence
- Provides open-access data through the SenNet Data Portal with standardized metadata
- Integrates with HuBMAP and other tissue atlasing initiatives for cross-reference
- Studies senescence in the context of aging, cancer, metabolic disease, and infection

## Use Cases in Research

- Investigating how cellular senescence contributes to chronic infection susceptibility and AMR
- Characterizing senescent immune cell populations in tissues affected by infectious disease
- Using SenNet spatial data to study senescence in gut epithelia relevant to microbiome interactions
- Integrating senescence signatures with other aging datasets for systems biology modeling
- Identifying senolytic or senomorphic drug targets relevant to infection-driven tissue damage

## Getting Started

Visit [SenNet](https://sennetconsortium.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
